rulings.Case Title: MERLYN SMITH VS. CERTAINEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Union Pacific , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 21, 2016 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Union Pacific Railroad Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff's action against defendant is time-barred pursuant to the applicable three-year statute of limitations. (45 U.S.C. § 56.) Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff knew, or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, of his asbestos-related illness(es) and their cause(s) more than three years prior to filing this action. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that summary judgment must be granted based on its argument that plaintiff does not currently suffer from any asbestos-related illness. Assuming that defendant's contention is true and that plaintiff currently has no asbestos-related illness, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff still cannot proceed with his action, as there is no dispute that at a minimum in the past plaintiff suffered from cancer. Even if defendant's argument could preclude plaintiff's action were correct, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not currently suffer from any asbestos-related illness. At a minimum, defendant did not present admissible evidence to demonstrate that plaintiff does not have the asbestos-related pleural disease alleged in his complaint. Assuming defendant had sustained its initial burden, Alvin Schonfeld's November 14, 2014 chest radiograph classification would create a triable issue whether plaintiff currently has an asbestos-related illness. Although defendant titled its motion as a motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication, the court treated it only as a motion for summary judgment, based on the arguments made by defendant, defendant's Separate Statement, and CRC 3.1350. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibit 1 (medical records) are overruled and his objections to Dr. Moscow's declaration are sustained. If the Court were to consider plaintiffs' evidence, which it does not, defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits D, E and F would be overruled. Based on this ruling, plaintiff's request made pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c(h) is moot. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
